title: "Heat_Wave"
output: html_document
date: "2025-08-03"

Dataset Setup
```{r}
library(readr)

df_wide <- read_csv(
  'data/private/NCD_Data_clean_categories.csv',
  skip = 4,
  locale = locale(encoding = "latin1")
)

serverity_df = read.csv('data/private/HW_DS_Severity_Scale.csv', skip = 0)


df_wide_copy = df_wide
df_wide <- df_wide[ , -3]
df_wide <- df_wide[1:(nrow(df_wide)-3), ]
df_wide = cbind(df_wide, serverity_df)
df_wide <- df_wide[, c(
  "T1_NCD_ID",
  "T1_D_Age",
  "T1_D_age_group",
  "T1_D_gender",
  "T1_D_family_size",
  "T1_D_monthly_income",
  "T1_L_household_area_categorical",

  "T1_M_clin_diagnosis",

  "T1_HW_9.1_impact_binary",
  "T1_HW_9.2_impact_descriptive...66",  # <-- use the suffixed name
  "T1_HW_symptom_severity_score",

  "T2_HW_2.1_impact_binary",
  "T2_HW_2.2_impact_descriptive",
  "T2_HW_symptom_severity_score",

  "T3_HW_2.1_impact_binary",
  "T3_HW_2.2_impact_descriptive",
  "T3_HW_symptom_severity_score"
)]
colnames(df_wide) = c("ID","Age","Age_Group","Gender","Family_Size","Monthly_Income","HH_Area","Prior_Diagnosis","T1_Heatwave_Affected","T1_Heatwave_Condition","T1_HW_symptom_severity_score","T2_Heatwave_Affected","T2_Heatwave_Condition","T2_HW_symptom_severity_score","T3_Heatwave_Affected","T3_Heatwave_Condition","T3_HW_symptom_severity_score")

library(dplyr)

df_wide$Monthly_Income <- df_wide$Monthly_Income |>
  # Replace all dash-like characters with normal dash
  (\(x) gsub("[\u0096–—]", "-", x))() |>
  # Remove extra spaces around dash
  (\(x) gsub("\\s*-\\s*", " - ", x))() |>
  # Fix "less than100"
  (\(x) gsub("less than ?100", "less than 100", x, ignore.case = TRUE))() |>
  # Trim leading/trailing spaces
  trimws() |>
  # Make factor
  (\(x) factor(x, levels = c(
    "less than 100 JD",
    "100 - 200 JD",
    "200 - 300 JD",
    "more than 300 JD"
  )))()


# Check the first few rows of the data
head(df_wide,10)
```




























Heatwave Condition Cleaning (remove affected but no condition, n = 12)
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Assuming your data frame is called df

rows_missing_symptoms <- df_wide %>%
  # Create a logical column for each wave: Affected == "Yes" & Condition is NA
  mutate(
    miss_T1 = (tolower(trimws(T1_Heatwave_Affected)) == "yes") & is.na(T1_Heatwave_Condition),
    miss_T2 = (tolower(trimws(T2_Heatwave_Affected)) == "yes") & is.na(T2_Heatwave_Condition),
    miss_T3 = (tolower(trimws(T3_Heatwave_Affected)) == "yes") & is.na(T3_Heatwave_Condition)
  ) %>%
  # Keep only rows where any are TRUE
  filter(miss_T1 | miss_T2 | miss_T3)

# Show the rows
print(rows_missing_symptoms)

# Filter out these IDs
df_wide <- df_wide %>%
  filter(!ID %in% rows_missing_symptoms$ID)

```

Clean Heatwave Condition
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

#T1,T2,T3_Heatwave_Cleaned_Condition
df_wide <- df_wide %>%
  mutate(
    T1_Heatwave_Cleaned_Condition = T1_Heatwave_Condition %>%
      str_to_upper() %>% str_squish() %>%
      str_replace_all("\\s*,\\s*", ",") %>%
      str_replace_all(",+", ",") %>%
      str_replace_all("\\s+", " "),
    T2_Heatwave_Cleaned_Condition = T2_Heatwave_Condition %>%
      str_to_upper() %>% str_squish() %>%
      str_replace_all("\\s*,\\s*", ",") %>%
      str_replace_all(",+", ",") %>%
      str_replace_all("\\s+", " "),
    T3_Heatwave_Cleaned_Condition = T3_Heatwave_Condition %>%
      str_to_upper() %>% str_squish() %>%
      str_replace_all("\\s*,\\s*", ",") %>%
      str_replace_all(",+", ",") %>%
      str_replace_all("\\s+", " "),
    Prior_Diagnosis_cleaned = Prior_Diagnosis %>%
      str_to_upper() %>%            # Convert to uppercase
      str_squish() %>%               # Trim extra spaces
      str_replace_all("\\s*,\\s*", ",") %>%  # Remove spaces around commas
      str_replace_all(",+", ",") %>%         # Remove multiple commas
      str_replace_all("\\s+", " ")           # Collapse multiple spaces
  )

head(df_wide)

```

Convert wide format to long format
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
# If a non-time-varying Affected exists, rename it so it won’t mask the pivot result
if ("Affected" %in% names(df_wide)) {
  df_wide <- df_wide %>% dplyr::rename(Affected_overall = Affected)
}

dat_long <- df_wide %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    # Grab Affected, Condition, and severity across T1/T2/T3
    cols = tidyselect::matches("^T[123]_(Heatwave_(Affected|Condition)|HW_symptom_severity_score)$"),
    names_to      = c("phase", ".value"),
    # Accept either "Heatwave_" or "HW_" and DROP that prefix
    names_pattern = "^T([123])_(?:Heatwave_|HW_)?(.*)$"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    phase = factor(paste0("T", phase), levels = c("T1","T2","T3")),
    Affected = case_when(
      is.na(Affected) ~ NA_real_,
      Affected %in% c(1, "1") ~ 1,
      Affected %in% c(0, "0") ~ 0,
      str_to_upper(str_trim(Affected)) %in% c("YES","Y","TRUE","T") ~ 1,
      str_to_upper(str_trim(Affected)) %in% c("NO","N","FALSE","F") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ) %>% as.integer()
  ) %>%
  # After the regex, columns will be: Affected, Condition, symptom_severity_score
  dplyr::select(
    ID, Age, Age_Group, Gender, Family_Size, Monthly_Income, HH_Area,
    phase, Affected, Condition, symptom_severity_score, Prior_Diagnosis
  )

# quick sanity checks
head(dat_long, 10)
count(dat_long, phase, !is.na(symptom_severity_score))


```

Rewrite the condition columns based on how you are affected
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# Your keyword lists
category_keywords <- list(
  Dyspnea              = c("DYSPNEA"),
  Fatigue              = c("FATIGUE"),
  Headache             = c("HEADACHE"),
  Sweating             = c("SWEATING"),
  Allergy              = c("ALLERGY"),
  Hypertension         = c("HYPERTENSION"),

  # Updated: HTN with all dataset variants
  HTN_prior         = c("HTN", "GESTATIONAL HTN"),

  # New: Asthma, Thyroid, and CVDs from your dataset
  Asthma_prior               = c("ASTHMA"),
  Thyroid_prior              = c("THYROID DISORDERS"),
  CVDs_prior                 = c(
    "CVD",
    "CVDS",
    "IHD",
    "HF",
    "CVDS(HF)",
    "CVDS(DVT)",
    "CVDS(HYPERTRIGLYCERIDMIA)",
    "CVDS(MVP)",
    "CVDS(CARDIAC VALVE REPLACEMENT)",
    "CVDS(CARDIOVASCULAR)",
    "CVDS ( AR GRADE II )",
    "CVDS ( CARDIOVASCULAR )"
  ),

  Respiratory          = c("COUGH", "TIGHTNESS", "CYANOSIS", "RESPIRATORY"),
  Neurologic           = c("CONVULSION", "NUMBNESS", "DIZZINESS", "NEUROLOGIC"),
  Gastrointestinal     = c("NAUSEA", "VOMITING", "ABDOMINAL PAIN", "GASTROINTESTINAL"),
  Psychological        = c("STRESS", "NERVOUS", "PSYCHOLOGICAL FATIGUE", "INSOMNIA", "PSYCHOLOGICAL"),
  Musculoskeletal      = c("JOINT PAIN", "FOOT PAIN", "MUSCULOSKELETAL"),
  Ocular               = c("REDNESS OF EYES", "EYE PAIN", "POOR VISION", "BLURRED VISION", "OCULAR"),
  Metabolic            = c("HYPERGLYCEMIA", "HYPOGLYCEMIA", "HYPERURICEMIA", "METABOLIC"),
  Cardiovascular       = c("HEART ATTACK", "CHEST PAIN", "PALPITATIONS", "TACHYCARDIA", "EDEMA",
                           "HYPERTENSION", "CARDIOVASCULAR"),
  Constitutional       = c("FEVER", "XEROSTOMIA", "RUNNY NOSE", "LACK OF APPETITE", "COMA",
                           "THIRSTY", "CONSTITUTIONAL")
)



process_both <- function(df, col1, col2, category_keywords) {
  # Ensure columns exist
  if (!all(c(col1, col2) %in% names(df))) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Missing columns: %s",
      paste(setdiff(c(col1, col2), names(df)), collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }

  n <- nrow(df)

  # to upper, normalize separators, split to tokens; keep length = n
  mk_list <- function(x) {
    x <- toupper(as.character(x))
    x[is.na(x)] <- ""
    # normalize separators: comma or slash -> comma
    x <- gsub("\\s*[,/]+\\s*", ",", x)
    # trim and split (always returns a list of length n)
    strsplit(trimws(x), ",", fixed = FALSE)
  }

  L1 <- mk_list(df[[col1]])
  L2 <- mk_list(df[[col2]])

  for (cat in names(category_keywords)) {
    kws <- toupper(category_keywords[[cat]])
    df[[cat]] <- vapply(seq_len(n), function(i) {
      vals <- unique(c(L1[[i]], L2[[i]]))
      vals <- vals[vals != ""]
      as.integer(any(vals %in% kws))
    }, integer(1))
  }
  df
}

# ✅ Use the correct column names produced by your pivot
dat_long <- process_both(
  dat_long,
  col1 = "Condition",
  col2 = "Prior_Diagnosis",
  category_keywords = category_keywords
)

# Manual QA correction from the working analysis was removed from this GitHub copy
# to avoid publishing a participant-specific study ID. Authorized analysts can
# apply the documented correction in the restricted data environment if needed.
head(dat_long,10)
```


Heatwave Model:
How does the probability that an individual NCD patient reports being affected by the heatwave change across different survey phases (T1, T2, T3), after accounting for repeated measurements within each person and adjusting for relevant covariates?

Participant_id = ID
Phase/Time = T1,T2,T3
Outcome : affected
Covariate = Age_Group, Gender

#Checking if Affected = 1 then Cleaned_Condition is not NA
#         if Affected = 0 then Cleaned_Condition is NA

```{r}
# Variables of interest
vars <- c("Dyspnea","Fatigue","Headache","Sweating","Allergy","Hypertension")

# Build summary table
out_list <- lapply(vars, function(v){
  tab <- table(dat_long[[v]])
  n0 <- ifelse("0" %in% names(tab), tab[["0"]], 0)
  n1 <- ifelse("1" %in% names(tab), tab[["1"]], 0)
  total <- n0 + n1
  data.frame(
    Outcome   = v,
    Count_0   = n0,
    Count_1   = n1,
    Percent_0 = round(100 * n0 / total, 1),
    Percent_1 = round(100 * n1 / total, 1)
  )
})

summary_tab <- do.call(rbind, out_list)
summary_tab

```

```{r}
  dat_long$Family_Size,
  breaks = c(0, 2, 5, 8, Inf),
  labels = c("1-2", "3-5", "6-8", "9+"),
  right = TRUE
)

table(dat_long$Family_Size_Group)


dat_long$Monthly_Income_Group <- ifelse(
  dat_long$Monthly_Income %in% c("200 - 300 JD", "more than 300 JD"),
  "200 JD or more",
  as.character(dat_long$Monthly_Income)
)

dat_long$Monthly_Income_Group <- factor(dat_long$Monthly_Income_Group,
                                        levels = c("less than 100 JD",

table(dat_long$Monthly_Income_Group)
```

Heatwave Model Selection (Chose Random Intercept)
```{r}
library(lme4)

#GLME Random Intercept
fit_RI <- glmer(
  Affected ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + Family_Size_Group + Monthly_Income_Group + HH_Area +
    HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + (1 | ID),
  family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)
summary(fit_RI)
print(fit_RI, correlation = TRUE)


#GLME Random Intercept and Slope
fit_RIS <- glmer(
  Affected ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + Family_Size + Monthly_Income + HH_Area +
     HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + (phase | ID),
  family = binomial,
  data = dat_long,
  nAGQ = 1,
  control = glmerControl(
    optimizer = "bobyqa",
    optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5)
  )
)
summary(fit_RIS)

AIC(fit_RI, fit_RIS)
```


Heatwave Model Selection: Choosing Time Trend (Categorical)
```{r}
# 0. Load packages
library(lme4)
library(splines)

# Response profile model (phase categorical)
fit_factor <- glmer(
  Affected ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + Family_Size_Group + Monthly_Income_Group +
    HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + (1 | ID),
  family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)

# Linear Time Effect
dat_long$phase_num <- as.numeric(as.factor(dat_long$phase)) - 1  # T1=0, T2=1, T3=2

fit_linear <- glmer(
  Affected ~ phase_num + Age_Group + Gender + Family_Size_Group + Monthly_Income_Group +
    HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + (1 | ID),
  family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20
)

# Quadratic Time Effect
dat_long$phase_num_c <- scale(dat_long$phase_num, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
fit_quadratic <- glmer(
  Affected ~ phase_num_c + I(phase_num_c^2) + Age_Group + Gender + Family_Size_Group +
    Monthly_Income_Group + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + (1 | ID),
  family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)


summary(fit_factor)
AIC(fit_factor, fit_linear, fit_quadratic)


```


Heatwave Income Level Linear Trend Test
```{r,eval=FALSE}
# make an ordered factor in the logical order
dat_long$Family_Size_Group_ord <- ordered(
  dat_long$Family_Size_Group,
  levels = c("1-2","3-5","6-8","9+")
)

# use orthogonal polynomial contrasts -> gives .L (linear), .Q (quadratic), .C (cubic if 4+ levels)
contrasts(dat_long$Family_Size_Group_ord) <- contr.poly(4)

# refit replacing the unordered factor with the ordered one
fit_trend <- update(fit_factor, . ~ . - Family_Size_Group + Family_Size_Group_ord)
summary(fit_trend)

```






Heatwave Phrase Testing does T2 drop to T3
```{r}
library(emmeans)
em  <- emmeans(fit_factor, ~ phase)
pr  <- contrast(em, method = list("T2 vs T3" = c(0, 1, -1)), adjust = "none")
summary(pr, type = "response", infer = c(TRUE, TRUE))  # OR, 95% CI, p

```


Assumption Function
```{r}
check_glmm_assumptions <- function(
  fit, data = NULL, alpha = 0.05,
  do_plots = FALSE,           # TRUE -> RE QQ plot
  do_sensitivity = TRUE       # GLM-CRSE & GEE comparisons
) {
  if (is.null(data)) data <- model.frame(fit)
  fm   <- formula(fit)
  mf   <- model.frame(fit)
  resp <- all.vars(fm)[1]
  idvar <- names(lme4::ranef(fit))[1]  # assumes single grouping var

  # ---------- helpers ----------
  overdisp_fun <- function(model) {
    rp  <- residuals(model, type = "pearson")
    rdf <- df.residual(model)
    chisq <- sum(rp^2)
    ratio <- chisq / rdf
    p     <- pchisq(chisq, df = rdf, lower.tail = FALSE)
    list(ratio = ratio, p = p)
  }

  # fixed-effect term labels (drop random terms)
  fe_terms <- attr(terms(fit), "term.labels")
  fe_terms <- fe_terms[!grepl("\\|", fe_terms)]
  fe_plain <- fe_terms[fe_terms %in% names(mf)]   # plain column names

  # ---------- 1) separation / sparse cells ----------
  sep_issues <- list()
  for (v in fe_plain) {
    if (is.factor(mf[[v]])) {
      tab <- table(mf[[v]], mf[[resp]])
      zero_lvls <- rownames(tab)[rowSums(tab == 0) > 0]
      if (length(zero_lvls)) sep_issues[[v]] <- zero_lvls
    }
  }
  sep_pass <- length(sep_issues) == 0

  # ---------- 2) multicollinearity (VIF on fixed-effects GLM) ----------
  vif_max <- NA; vif_tbl <- NULL; vif_pass <- NA
  if (requireNamespace("car", quietly = TRUE)) {
    fe_form <- stats::reformulate(fe_terms, response = resp)
    glm_fix <- stats::glm(fe_form, family = binomial, data = data)
    vif_tbl <- tryCatch(car::vif(glm_fix), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(vif_tbl)) {
      vif_max <- if (is.matrix(vif_tbl)) max(vif_tbl[,1]) else max(vif_tbl)
      vif_pass <- is.finite(vif_max) && (vif_max < 5)
    }
  }

  # ---------- 3) linearity of logit for numeric FE ----------
  nums <- fe_plain[sapply(mf[fe_plain], is.numeric)]
  nonlin <- list()
  if (length(nums)) {
    for (v in nums) {
      f2 <- stats::update.formula(fm, paste0(". ~ . + I(", v, "^2)"))
      m2 <- tryCatch(lme4::update(fit, formula = f2), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (!is.null(m2)) {
        a <- suppressWarnings(anova(fit, m2, test = "Chisq"))
        p <- tryCatch(a$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2], error = function(e) NA)
        if (is.finite(p) && p < alpha) nonlin[[v]] <- p
      }
    }
  }
  linear_pass <- length(nonlin) == 0

  # ---------- 4) overdispersion ----------
  od <- overdisp_fun(fit)
  od_pass <- is.finite(od$ratio) && (od$ratio < 1.2)

  # ---------- 5) random-effects normality (+ optional QQ plot) ----------
  re_norm_p <- NA; re_pass <- NA
  RE <- lme4::ranef(fit)
  if (length(RE)) {
    revec <- tryCatch(as.numeric(RE[[1]][,"(Intercept)"]), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(revec) && length(revec) >= 3) {
      re_norm_p <- tryCatch(stats::shapiro.test(revec)$p.value, error = function(e) NA)
      re_pass   <- if (is.finite(re_norm_p)) re_norm_p > 0.05 else NA
      if (isTRUE(do_plots)) {
        qqnorm(revec, main = "Random-effects (ID intercept) QQ plot"); qqline(revec)
      }
    } else re_pass <- TRUE
  }

  # singular (boundary) fit?
  singular_flag <- lme4::isSingular(fit)

  # ---------- 6) DHARMa residual diagnostics ----------
  dh_p_unif <- dh_p_disp <- dh_p_out <- NA; dh_pass <- NA
  if (requireNamespace("DHARMa", quietly = TRUE)) {
    sim <- DHARMa::simulateResiduals(fit, n = 1000)
    dh_p_unif <- tryCatch(DHARMa::testUniformity(sim)$p.value, error = function(e) NA)
    dh_p_disp <- tryCatch(DHARMa::testDispersion(sim)$p.value,  error = function(e) NA)
    dh_p_out  <- tryCatch(DHARMa::testOutliers(sim)$p.value,    error = function(e) NA)
    dh_pass <- all(is.finite(c(dh_p_unif, dh_p_disp, dh_p_out)) &
                     c(dh_p_unif, dh_p_disp, dh_p_out) > 0.05)
  }

  # ---------- 7) R2 (context) ----------
  r2_marg <- r2_cond <- NA
  if (requireNamespace("MuMIn", quietly = TRUE)) {
    r2 <- suppressWarnings(MuMIn::r.squaredGLMM(fit))
    r2_marg <- as.numeric(r2[1]); r2_cond <- as.numeric(r2[2])
  }

  # ---------- 8) Sensitivity analyses (optional) ----------
  sens <- list()
  if (isTRUE(do_sensitivity)) {
    fe_form <- stats::reformulate(fe_terms, response = resp)

    # build a consistent model frame containing response + fixed terms + ID
    cols <- unique(c(resp, fe_terms, idvar))
    mf2  <- stats::model.frame(
      stats::as.formula(paste(resp, "~", paste(fe_terms, collapse = " + "))),
      data = data, na.action = stats::na.omit
    )
    # add ID, then drop rows with NA in ID as well
    mf2[[idvar]] <- data[[idvar]][as.integer(row.names(mf2))]
    mf2 <- stats::na.omit(mf2)

    # GLM + cluster-robust SE (ID)
    if (requireNamespace("sandwich", quietly = TRUE) &&
        requireNamespace("lmtest", quietly = TRUE)) {
      glm0 <- stats::glm(fe_form, family = binomial, data = mf2)
      Vcl  <- sandwich::vcovCL(glm0, cluster = mf2[[idvar]])
      sens$glm_crse <- lmtest::coeftest(glm0, vcov = Vcl)
    }

    # GEE (population-averaged)
    if (requireNamespace("geepack", quietly = TRUE)) {
      # ensure ID is a factor and each cluster has >=1 obs (geepack can handle singletons)
      mf2[[idvar]] <- as.factor(mf2[[idvar]])
      sens$gee <- tryCatch(
        {
          gee_fit <- geepack::geeglm(
            fe_form, id = mf2[[idvar]], data = mf2,
            family = binomial, corstr = "exchangeable"
          )
          summary(gee_fit)$coefficients
        },
        error = function(e) { attr(e, "message") <- conditionMessage(e); e }
      )
    }

    # agreement vs GLMM (sign + significance) for overlapping terms
    glmm_coef <- lme4::fixef(fit)
    glmm_se   <- sqrt(diag(vcov(fit)))
    glmm_p    <- 2*pnorm(abs(glmm_coef/glmm_se), lower.tail = FALSE)
    keep <- names(glmm_coef)[names(glmm_coef)!="(Intercept)"]

    agree <- list()
    if (!is.null(sens$glm_crse)) {
      cr <- sens$glm_crse
      common <- intersect(keep, rownames(cr))
      same_sign <- mean(sign(glmm_coef[common]) == sign(cr[common,1]))
      flip_sig  <- sum((glmm_p[common] < alpha) != (cr[common,4] < alpha))
      agree$glm_crse <- list(same_sign = same_sign, flips = flip_sig, n = length(common))
    }
    if (!inherits(sens$gee, "error") && !is.null(sens$gee)) {
      ge <- sens$gee
      common <- intersect(keep, rownames(ge))
      pgee <- 2*pnorm(abs(ge[common,"Estimate"]/ge[common,"Std.err"]), lower.tail = FALSE)
      same_sign <- mean(sign(glmm_coef[common]) == sign(ge[common,"Estimate"]))
      flip_sig  <- sum((glmm_p[common] < alpha) != (pgee < alpha))
      agree$gee <- list(same_sign = same_sign, flips = flip_sig, n = length(common))
    }
    sens$agreement <- agree
  }
  # ---------- summary & verdict ----------
  res <- list(
    separation_pass = sep_pass, separation_details = sep_issues,
    vif_pass = vif_pass, vif_max = vif_max, vif_table = vif_tbl,
    linearity_pass = linear_pass, linearity_details = nonlin,
    overdisp_pass = od_pass, overdisp_ratio = od$ratio, overdisp_p = od$p,
    re_normal_pass = re_pass, re_shapiro_p = re_norm_p,
    singular_fit = singular_flag,
    dharma_pass = dh_pass, dharma_p = c(uniformity = dh_p_unif, dispersion = dh_p_disp, outliers = dh_p_out),
    r2_marginal = r2_marg, r2_conditional = r2_cond,
    sensitivity = sens
  )

  core_flags <- c(
    sep_pass,
    ifelse(is.na(vif_pass), TRUE, vif_pass),
    linear_pass,
    od_pass,
    ifelse(is.na(re_pass), TRUE, re_pass),
    ifelse(is.na(dh_pass), TRUE, dh_pass),
    !isTRUE(singular_flag) # treat singular as a fail/caution
  )
  overall <- all(core_flags)

  cat("\nGLMM assumption check (logit, random intercept)\n")
  cat("------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(sprintf("Separation/sparse cells .......... %s\n", if (sep_pass) "OK ✓" else "ISSUE ✗"))
  if (!sep_pass && length(sep_issues)) print(sep_issues)

  cat(sprintf("Multicollinearity (max VIF) ....... %s",
              if (is.na(vif_max)) "NA (car not available)" else sprintf("%.2f", vif_max)))
  cat(sprintf("  -> %s\n", if (isTRUE(vif_pass)) "OK ✓" else if (is.na(vif_pass)) "" else "High VIF ✗"))

  cat(sprintf("Linearity of logit (numeric) ...... %s\n",
              if (linear_pass) "OK ✓" else "Nonlinearity detected ✗"))

  cat(sprintf("Overdispersion ratio .............. %.2f  -> %s\n",
              od$ratio, if (od_pass) "OK ✓" else "Possible overdispersion ✗"))

  cat(sprintf("RE normality (Shapiro p) .......... %s  -> %s\n",
              if (is.na(re_norm_p)) "NA" else sprintf("%.3f", re_norm_p),
              if (isTRUE(re_pass)) "OK ✓" else if (is.na(re_pass)) "" else "Deviates ✗"))

  cat(sprintf("Singular (boundary) fit ........... %s\n", if (singular_flag) "YES ⚠️" else "NO ✓"))

  cat(sprintf("DHARMa (uniform/disp/outliers p) .. %s  -> %s\n",
              paste(ifelse(is.na(dh_p_unif), "NA",
                           sprintf("%.3f/%.3f/%.3f", dh_p_unif, dh_p_disp, dh_p_out))),
              if (isTRUE(dh_pass)) "OK ✓" else if (is.na(dh_pass)) "Skipped" else "Issue ✗"))

  cat(sprintf("R2 (marginal / conditional) ....... %s / %s\n",
              if (is.na(r2_marg)) "NA" else sprintf("%.3f", r2_marg),
              if (is.na(r2_cond)) "NA" else sprintf("%.3f", r2_cond)))

  if (isTRUE(do_sensitivity)) {
    cat("Sensitivity (agreement vs GLMM):\n")
    if (!is.null(sens$agreement$glm_crse)) {
      a <- sens$agreement$glm_crse
      cat(sprintf("  GLM-CRSE ... same sign = %.0f%%, flips = %d/%d\n",
                  100*a$same_sign, a$flips, a$n))
    } else cat("  GLM-CRSE ... skipped (packages unavailable)\n")
    if (!is.null(sens$agreement$gee)) {
      a <- sens$agreement$gee
      cat(sprintf("  GEE ........ same sign = %.0f%%, flips = %d/%d\n",
                  100*a$same_sign, a$flips, a$n))
    } else cat("  GEE ........ skipped (packages unavailable)\n")
  }

  cat("------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(sprintf("Overall verdict: %s\n\n", if (overall) "Model is OK to present ✅" else "Needs attention ⚠️"))

  res$overall_ok <- overall
  invisible(res)
}

assess <- check_glmm_assumptions(fit_factor, data = dat_long,
                                 do_plots = TRUE, do_sensitivity = TRUE)

assess$overall_ok
assess$sensitivity$agreement


```


We fit a mixed-effects logistic regression with a random intercept for participants. Model diagnostics (DHARMa uniformity/dispersion/outlier tests, overdispersion, sparse cells, and VIF) indicated adequate fit and no problematic collinearity. The distribution of participant-level BLUPs deviated from normality by Shapiro’s test (expected with large cluster counts and because BLUPs are not the random effects themselves), but QQ plots suggested mild deviations only. Results were robust in sensitivity analyses using (i) logistic regression with cluster-robust SE and (ii) GEE with exchangeable correlation: signs and significance were unchanged.





Heatwave GEE Model (Model Selection and Assumption Check)
Heatwave GEE Final Model
```{r}
# ===========================
# GEE selection + assumptions (Affected outcome) - T1/T2/T3 coding
# ===========================
suppressPackageStartupMessages({ library(geepack); library(emmeans) })

# ---- 0) Clean & sort --------------------------------------------
# use HEATWAVE long data
base_df <- dat_long   # <--- use dat_long (not dat_long_ds)

# allow for grouped or raw versions of family size & income
fam_sz <- if ("Family_Size_Group" %in% names(base_df)) "Family_Size_Group" else "Family_Size"
inc    <- if ("Monthly_Income_Group" %in% names(base_df)) "Monthly_Income_Group" else "Monthly_Income"

vars <- c("Affected","phase","Age_Group","Gender", fam_sz, inc,
          "HH_Area","HTN_prior","Asthma_prior","Thyroid_prior","ID")

d <- na.omit(base_df[, vars])
d <- droplevels(d)
stopifnot(!anyNA(d$ID))

# Robust 0/1 coercion for the binary outcome
to01 <- function(x){
  if (is.logical(x)) return(as.integer(x))
  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    ux <- sort(unique(x))
    if (all(ux %in% c(0,1))) return(as.integer(x))
    stop("Numeric Affected must be coded 0/1; found: ", paste(ux, collapse=", "))
  }
  if (is.factor(x) || is.character(x)) {
    z <- tolower(trimws(as.character(x)))
    ones  <- c("1","yes","y","true","t","present","pos","positive")
    zeros <- c("0","no","n","false","f","absent","neg","negative")
    out <- ifelse(z %in% ones, 1L, ifelse(z %in% zeros, 0L, NA_integer_))
    if (any(is.na(out))) {
      bad <- sort(unique(z[is.na(out)]))
      stop("Unrecognized Affected levels for 0/1 mapping: ", paste(bad, collapse=", "))
    }
    return(out)
  }
  stop("Unsupported Affected type: ", class(x)[1])
}
d$Affected <- to01(d$Affected)

# Use treatment contrasts with T1 as reference (UNORDERED)
want_lvls <- c("T1","T2","T3")
if (!all(want_lvls %in% levels(d$phase))) {
  stop("phase must have levels T1, T2, T3; found: ", paste(levels(d$phase), collapse=", "))
}
d$phase <- factor(d$phase, levels = want_lvls, ordered = FALSE)
contrasts(d$phase) <- stats::contr.treatment(n = length(levels(d$phase)), base = 1)

# AR(1) wave index + sort
d$wave <- as.integer(d$phase)
d <- d[order(d$ID, d$phase), ]

# Common formula
form <- as.formula(sprintf(
  "Affected ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + %s + %s + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior",
  fam_sz, inc
))

# ---- 1) Fit grid: working correlation × link ---------------------
cor_opts <- c("independence","exchangeable","ar1","unstructured")
families <- list(
  logit   = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),
  probit  = stats::binomial(link = "probit"),
  cloglog = stats::binomial(link = "cloglog")
)

fits <- list(); rows <- list()
for (cc in cor_opts) {
  for (lname in names(families)) {
    fam <- families[[lname]]
    key <- paste(cc, lname, sep = "_")
    fit <- geeglm(form, id = ID, waves = wave, family = fam, corstr = cc, data = d)
    qic <- geepack::QIC(fit)
    fits[[key]] <- fit
    rows[[key]] <- data.frame(Correlation = cc, Link = lname,
                              QIC = unname(qic[["QIC"]]),
                              QICu = if (!is.null(qic[["QICu"]])) unname(qic[["QICu"]]) else NA_real_)
  }
}
sel_tab <- do.call(rbind, rows); row.names(sel_tab) <- NULL
sel_tab <- sel_tab[order(sel_tab$QIC), ]
cat("\nModel selection grid (lower QIC is better):\n"); print(sel_tab)

best_idx  <- which.min(sel_tab$QIC)
best_cor  <- sel_tab$Correlation[best_idx]
best_link <- sel_tab$Link[best_idx]
best_gee_model <- fits[[paste(best_cor, best_link, sep = "_")]]
cat("\nBest: correlation =", best_cor, ", link =", best_link,
    " (QIC =", round(sel_tab$QIC[best_idx], 3),
    if (!is.na(sel_tab$QICu[best_idx])) paste0(", QICu = ", round(sel_tab$QICu[best_idx], 3)) else "",
    ")\n")
summary(best_gee_model)

# ---- 2) Slide-style table (logit only) ---------------------------
if (!is.null(fits[["unstructured_logit"]])) {
  qic_log <- c(
    Unstructured              = geepack::QIC(fits[["unstructured_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    `Constant (Exchangeable)` = geepack::QIC(fits[["exchangeable_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    `AR(1)`                   = geepack::QIC(fits[["ar1_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    Independence              = geepack::QIC(fits[["independence_logit"]])[["QIC"]]
  )
  pres_tab <- data.frame(Correlation = names(qic_log),
                         `QIC (logit)` = round(unname(qic_log), 1),
                         check.names = FALSE)
  cat("\nWorking correlation comparison (logit only):\n"); print(pres_tab)
}

# ---- 3) Diagnostics ----------------------------------------------
n_clusters <- length(unique(d$ID))
cat("\n# Clusters:", n_clusters, "\n")
cat("Cluster-size distribution:\n"); print(table(table(d$ID)))

wcv <- tapply(d$phase, d$ID, function(x) length(unique(x)))
cat("Proportion of clusters with >1 phase level:", round(mean(wcv > 1), 3), "\n")

tab_phase <- xtabs(~ Affected + phase, data = d)
cat("\nContingency of Affected by phase:\n"); print(tab_phase)
if (any(apply(tab_phase, 2, function(col) any(col == 0)))) {
  warning("Zero cell(s) detected for some phase levels (check separation).")
}

phi <- sum(residuals(best_gee_model, type = "pearson")^2) / (nrow(d) - length(coef(best_gee_model)))
cat("\nPearson scale (phi):", round(phi, 3), " ( >1 suggests overdispersion )\n")

if (!is.null(best_gee_model$geese$alpha)) {
  cat("Estimated working correlation (alpha):", round(best_gee_model$geese$alpha, 3), "\n")
}

phase_terms <- grep("^phase", names(coef(best_gee_model)), value = TRUE)
if (length(phase_terms) > 0) {
  target   <- phase_terms[1]
  base_est <- coef(best_gee_model)[target]
  ids <- unique(d$ID)
  set.seed(1)
  probe_ids <- if (length(ids) > 80) sample(ids, 80) else ids

  loo_delta <- sapply(probe_ids, function(i){
    dd <- d[d$ID != i, ]
    dd <- dd[order(dd$ID, dd$phase), ]
    fam_best <- families[[best_link]]
    fit_i <- geeglm(form, id = ID, waves = wave, family = fam_best, corstr = best_cor, data = dd)
    cf <- coef(fit_i); if (!target %in% names(cf)) return(NA_real_)
    cf[target] - base_est
  })
  infl_tab <- data.frame(ID = probe_ids, dBeta = loo_delta)
  infl_tab <- infl_tab[order(abs(infl_tab$dBeta), decreasing = TRUE), ]
  cat("\nTop 10 influential clusters for", target, "(|Δβ|):\n")
  print(head(infl_tab, 10), row.names = FALSE)
}

# ---- 4) Effects table with robust CIs ----------------------------
est <- coef(best_gee_model)
se  <- sqrt(diag(best_gee_model$geese$vbeta))
z   <- est / se
p   <- 2*pnorm(abs(z), lower.tail = FALSE)

if (best_link == "logit") {
  OUT <- data.frame(
    Variable = names(est),
    OR  = round(exp(est), 3),
    LCL = round(exp(est - 1.96*se), 3),
    UCL = round(exp(est + 1.96*se), 3),
    `p(Wald)` = signif(p, 3),
    check.names = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
} else {
  OUT <- data.frame(
    Variable = names(est),
    Estimate = round(est, 3),
    LCL = round(est - 1.96*se, 3),
    UCL = round(est + 1.96*se, 3),
    `p(Wald)` = signif(p, 3),
    Link = best_link,
    check.names = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
}
cat("\nPopulation-averaged effects for best GEE model (Affected):\n"); print(OUT)

# ---- 5) Pairwise phase contrasts (T2–T1, T3–T1, T3–T2) -----------
emm  <- emmeans(best_gee_model, ~ phase, vcov. = vcov(best_gee_model))
cmp  <- pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")
cat("\nPairwise phase contrasts (", best_link, " link):\n", sep = ""); print(summary(cmp))

if (best_link == "logit") {
  sc <- summary(cmp)
  OR_tab <- transform(sc,
    OR  = exp(estimate),
    LCL = exp(estimate - 1.96*SE),
    UCL = exp(estimate + 1.96*SE)
  )[ , c("contrast","OR","LCL","UCL","p.value")]
  cat("\nPairwise odds ratios (logit link):\n"); print(OR_tab, row.names = FALSE)
}

cat("\nCoefficient names:\n"); print(names(coef(best_gee_model)))

```



















Heatwave Condition
1.Dyspnea LGME (RI)
```{r}
# ============================
# GLMM (LGME) — Dyspnea outcome (HEATWAVE, dat_long)
# Rules:
#   - Fit RI models only
#   - Compute AIC for time effects (factor, linear, quadratic)
#   - Always select the factor time model
# ============================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(lme4)
  library(emmeans)      # pairwise contrasts for factor time
  library(performance)  # check_glmm_assumptions()
})

## --- Prep ---
stopifnot(all(c("Dyspnea","phase","ID") %in% names(dat_long)))

# Ensure 0/1 outcome
to01 <- function(x){
  if (is.logical(x)) return(as.integer(x))
  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    ux <- sort(unique(x))
    if (all(ux %in% c(0,1))) return(as.integer(x))
    stop("Numeric Dyspnea must be coded 0/1; found: ", paste(ux, collapse=", "))
  }
  if (is.factor(x) || is.character(x)) {
    z <- tolower(trimws(as.character(x)))
    ones  <- c("1","yes","y","true","t","present","pos","positive")
    zeros <- c("0","no","n","false","f","absent","neg","negative")
    out <- ifelse(z %in% ones, 1L, ifelse(z %in% zeros, 0L, NA_integer_))
    if (any(is.na(out))) {
      bad <- sort(unique(z[is.na(out)]))
      stop("Unrecognized Dyspnea levels for 0/1 mapping: ", paste(bad, collapse=", "))
    }
    return(out)
  }
  stop("Unsupported Dyspnea type: ", class(x)[1])
}
base_df <- dat_long
base_df$Dyspnea <- to01(base_df$Dyspnea)

# Phase as categorical T1/T2/T3 (T1 baseline, UNORDERED)
want_lvls <- c("T1","T2","T3")
base_df$phase <- factor(base_df$phase, levels = want_lvls, ordered = FALSE)
op_old <- options(contrasts = c("contr.treatment","contr.treatment"))

# Allow grouped/raw versions for family size & income
fam_sz <- if ("Family_Size_Group" %in% names(base_df)) "Family_Size_Group" else "Family_Size"
inc    <- if ("Monthly_Income_Group" %in% names(base_df)) "Monthly_Income_Group" else "Monthly_Income"

# Numeric encodings for time
base_df$phase_num   <- as.numeric(base_df$phase) - 1
base_df$phase_num_c <- scale(base_df$phase_num, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)

ctrl_RI <- glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))

## --- 1) RI models with different time parameterizations ---

# (a) Factor (response profile: T2/T3 vs T1)
fit_factor_dyspnea <- glmer(
  as.formula(paste0(
    "Dyspnea ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
    " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)"
  )),
  family = binomial, data = base_df, nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI
)

# (b) Linear time (0→1→2)
fit_linear_dyspnea <- glmer(
  as.formula(paste0(
    "Dyspnea ~ phase_num + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
    " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)"
  )),
  family = binomial, data = base_df, nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI
)

# (c) Quadratic time (centered linear + square)
fit_quadratic_dyspnea <- glmer(
  as.formula(paste0(
    "Dyspnea ~ phase_num_c + I(phase_num_c^2) + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
    " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)"
  )),
  family = binomial, data = base_df, nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI
)

cat("\n=== AIC: time specifications (Dyspnea, RI only) ===\n")
aic_time <- data.frame(
  TimeSpec = c("factor","linear","quadratic"),
  AIC      = c(AIC(fit_factor_dyspnea), AIC(fit_linear_dyspnea), AIC(fit_quadratic_dyspnea))
)
print(aic_time[order(aic_time$AIC), ])

# Forced choice = factor model
best_timespec <- "factor"
best_glmm_RI  <- fit_factor_dyspnea
cat("\n>> Forced selection — Time effect: ", best_timespec, " (reporting factor model)\n", sep = "")

## --- 2) Assumption check on selected model -----------------------
assess_RI <- check_glmm_assumptions(
  best_glmm_RI,
  data = base_df,
  do_plots = TRUE,
  do_sensitivity = TRUE
)
cat("\nAssumption check — overall OK? ", assess_RI$overall_ok, "\n")
cat("Sensitivity agreement (GLM-CRSE, GEE):\n")
print(assess_RI$sensitivity$agreement)

## --- 3) Final summary of the selected model ----------------------
cat("\n=== Summary of the selected RI model (Dyspnea, factor time) ===\n")
print(summary(best_glmm_RI))

## --- 4) Pairwise phase contrasts (always run since factor selected) ---
cat("\nPairwise phase contrasts (log-odds), Holm-adjusted:\n")
emm_fac <- emmeans(best_glmm_RI, ~ phase)
print(pairs(emm_fac, adjust = "holm"))

cat("\nSame contrasts as odds ratios (95% CI):\n")
print(summary(pairs(emm_fac, adjust = "holm"), type = "response", infer = c(TRUE, TRUE)))

## (Optional) restore previous contrast settings
options(op_old)

```


1.Heatwave Dyspnea GEE
```{r}
# ===========================
# GEE selection + assumptions (Dyspnea outcome) - T1/T2/T3 coding
# ===========================
suppressPackageStartupMessages({ library(geepack); library(emmeans) })

# ---- 0) Clean & sort --------------------------------------------
vars <- c("Dyspnea","phase","Age_Group","Gender","Family_Size_Group",
          "Monthly_Income_Group","HH_Area","HTN_prior","Asthma_prior",
          "Thyroid_prior","CVDs_prior","ID")

d <- na.omit(dat_long[, vars])
d <- droplevels(d)
stopifnot(!anyNA(d$ID))

# ensure binary 0/1
d$Dyspnea <- as.integer(d$Dyspnea)

# >>> IMPORTANT: use treatment contrasts (T1 reference), NOT polynomial L/Q
d$phase <- factor(d$phase, levels = c("T1","T2","T3"), ordered = FALSE)
# (Optional but safe) set global contrasts for this session:
# options(contrasts = c("contr.treatment","contr.poly"))

# Sort so AR(1) can infer spacing by row order (if chosen)
d <- d[order(d$ID, d$phase), ]

# Common formula (same covariates as your GLMM)
form <- Dyspnea ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + Family_Size_Group + Monthly_Income_Group +
  HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior

# ---- 1) Fit grid: working correlation × link ---------------------
cor_opts <- c("independence","exchangeable","ar1","unstructured")
families <- list(
  logit   = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),
  probit  = stats::binomial(link = "probit"),
  cloglog = stats::binomial(link = "cloglog")
)

fits <- list(); rows <- list()
for (cc in cor_opts) {
  for (lname in names(families)) {
    fam <- families[[lname]]
    key <- paste(cc, lname, sep = "_")
    fit <- geeglm(form, id = ID, family = fam, corstr = cc, data = d)
    qic <- geepack::QIC(fit)
    fits[[key]] <- fit
    rows[[key]] <- data.frame(Correlation = cc, Link = lname,
                              QIC = unname(qic[["QIC"]]),
                              QICu = if (!is.null(qic[["QICu"]])) unname(qic[["QICu"]]) else NA_real_)
  }
}
sel_tab <- do.call(rbind, rows)
row.names(sel_tab) <- NULL
sel_tab <- sel_tab[order(sel_tab$QIC), ]
cat("\nModel selection grid (lower QIC is better):\n"); print(sel_tab)

best_idx  <- which.min(sel_tab$QIC)
best_cor  <- sel_tab$Correlation[best_idx]
best_link <- sel_tab$Link[best_idx]
fit_best  <- fits[[paste(best_cor, best_link, sep = "_")]]

cat("\nBest: correlation =", best_cor, ", link =", best_link,
    " (QIC =", round(sel_tab$QIC[best_idx], 3),
    if (!is.na(sel_tab$QICu[best_idx])) paste0(", QICu = ", round(sel_tab$QICu[best_idx], 3)) else "",
    ")\n")

# ---- 2) Slide-style table (logit only) ---------------------------
if (!is.null(fits[["unstructured_logit"]])) {
  qic_log <- c(
    Unstructured              = geepack::QIC(fits[["unstructured_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    `Constant (Exchangeable)` = geepack::QIC(fits[["exchangeable_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    `AR(1)`                   = geepack::QIC(fits[["ar1_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    Independence              = geepack::QIC(fits[["independence_logit"]])[["QIC"]]
  )
  pres_tab <- data.frame(Correlation = names(qic_log),
                         `QIC (logit)` = round(unname(qic_log), 1),
                         check.names = FALSE)
  cat("\nWorking correlation comparison (logit only):\n"); print(pres_tab)
}

# ---- 3) Diagnostics ----------------------------------------------
# 3a) Clusters & size
n_clusters <- length(unique(d$ID))
cat("\n# Clusters:", n_clusters, "\n")
cat("Cluster-size distribution:\n"); print(table(table(d$ID)))

# 3b) Within-ID variation for 'phase'
wcv <- tapply(d$phase, d$ID, function(x) length(unique(x)))
cat("Proportion of clusters with >1 phase level:", round(mean(wcv > 1), 3), "\n")

# 3c) Sparse cells / separation check for phase
tab_phase <- xtabs(~ Dyspnea + phase, data = d)
cat("\nContingency of Dyspnea by phase:\n"); print(tab_phase)

# 3d) Overdispersion (binomial quasi-likelihood)
phi <- sum(residuals(fit_best, type = "pearson")^2) / (nrow(d) - length(coef(fit_best)))
cat("\nPearson scale (phi):", round(phi, 3), " ( >1 suggests overdispersion )\n")

# 3e) Estimated working correlation
if (!is.null(fit_best$geese$alpha)) {
  cat("Estimated working correlation (alpha):", round(fit_best$geese$alpha, 3), "\n")
}

# 3f) Influence: leave-one-ID-out on first 'phase' term (T2 vs T1 coefficient)
phase_terms <- grep("^phase", names(coef(fit_best)), value = TRUE)
if (length(phase_terms) > 0) {
  target   <- phase_terms[1]   # typically "phaseT2" if T1 is reference
  base_est <- coef(fit_best)[target]
  ids <- unique(d$ID)
  set.seed(1)
  probe_ids <- if (length(ids) > 80) sample(ids, 80) else ids

  loo_delta <- sapply(probe_ids, function(i){
    dd <- d[d$ID != i, ]
    dd <- dd[order(dd$ID, dd$phase), ]
    fam_best <- families[[best_link]]
    fit_i <- geeglm(form, id = ID, family = fam_best, corstr = best_cor, data = dd)
    cf <- coef(fit_i); if (!target %in% names(cf)) return(NA_real_)
    cf[target] - base_est
  })
  infl_tab <- data.frame(ID = probe_ids, dBeta = loo_delta)
  infl_tab <- infl_tab[order(abs(infl_tab$dBeta), decreasing = TRUE), ]
  cat("\nTop 10 influential clusters for", target, "(|Δβ|):\n")
  print(head(infl_tab, 10), row.names = FALSE)
}

# ---- 4) Effects table with robust CIs ----------------------------
est <- coef(fit_best)
se  <- sqrt(diag(fit_best$geese$vbeta))     # robust (sandwich)
z   <- est / se
p   <- 2*pnorm(abs(z), lower.tail = FALSE)

if (best_link == "logit") {
  OUT <- data.frame(
    Variable = names(est),
    OR  = round(exp(est), 3),
    LCL = round(exp(est - 1.96*se), 3),
    UCL = round(exp(est + 1.96*se), 3),
    `p(Wald)` = signif(p, 3),
    check.names = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
} else {
  OUT <- data.frame(
    Variable = names(est),
    Estimate = round(est, 3),
    LCL = round(est - 1.96*se),  # keep on link scale
    UCL = round(est + 1.96*se),
    `p(Wald)` = signif(p, 3),
    Link = best_link,
    check.names = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
}
cat("\nPopulation-averaged effects for best GEE model:\n"); print(OUT)

# ---- 5) Pairwise phase contrasts (T2–T1, T3–T1, T3–T2) -----------
emm  <- emmeans(fit_best, ~ phase, vcov. = vcov(fit_best))   # robust vcov from GEE
cmp  <- pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")
cat("\nPairwise phase contrasts (", best_link, " link):\n", sep = ""); print(summary(cmp))

# Also show odds ratios if the chosen link is logit
if (best_link == "logit") {
  sc <- summary(cmp)
  OR_tab <- transform(sc,
    OR  = exp(estimate),
    LCL = exp(estimate - 1.96*SE),
    UCL = exp(estimate + 1.96*SE)
  )[ , c("contrast","OR","LCL","UCL","p.value")]
  cat("\nPairwise odds ratios (logit link):\n"); print(OR_tab, row.names = FALSE)
}



```

1.Heatwave Dyspnea GEE
```{r}
# From your selection step:
best_link <- "cloglog"
best_cor  <- "unstructured"

family_map <- list(
  logit   = binomial("logit"),
  probit  = binomial("probit"),
  cloglog = binomial("cloglog")
)

# Same 'form' and 'd' you built above, with Dyspnea as outcome
best_gee_model <- geeglm(
  form, id = ID,
  family = family_map[[best_link]],   # cloglog
  corstr = best_cor,                  # unstructured
  data   = d
)

summary(best_gee_model)
```

2.Heatwave Fatigue LGME (RIS)
Dont use this, although RIS givs lower AIC, but cant pass the assumption
```{r,eval=FALSE}
## ============================
## GLMM selector for Fatigue (T1/T2/T3 coding)
## ============================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(lme4)
  library(splines)
  library(emmeans)   # optional; for pairwise phase contrasts
})

## ---------- Prep ----------
stopifnot(all(c("Fatigue","phase","ID") %in% names(dat_long)))
dat_long$Fatigue <- as.integer(dat_long$Fatigue)

# Phase as UNordered factor (no polynomial coding), T1 is reference
dat_long$phase <- factor(dat_long$phase, levels = c("T1","T2","T3"), ordered = FALSE)

# Force treatment contrasts globally (fixed & random parts)
op_old <- options(contrasts = c("contr.treatment","contr.treatment"))

# Be tolerant to either grouped or raw names for these covariates
fam_sz <- if ("Family_Size_Group" %in% names(dat_long)) "Family_Size_Group" else "Family_Size"
inc    <- if ("Monthly_Income_Group" %in% names(dat_long)) "Monthly_Income_Group" else "Monthly_Income"

# Numeric time encodings (for linear/quadratic candidate models)
dat_long$phase_num   <- as.numeric(dat_long$phase) - 1            # 0,1,2 with T1=0
dat_long$phase_num_c <- scale(dat_long$phase_num, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)

# Controls
ctrl_RI  <- glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
ctrl_RIS <- glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 5e5))

## ---------- Step 1: RI vs RIS using factor time ----------
form_RI_factor <- as.formula(
  paste0("Fatigue ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
)
fit_RI_fatigue <- glmer(form_RI_factor, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

# With treatment contrasts, (phase | ID) uses dummy slopes for T2, T3 (ref = T1) — no L/Q
form_RIS_factor <- as.formula(
  paste0("Fatigue ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (phase | ID)")
)
fit_RIS_fatigue <- glmer(form_RIS_factor, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 1, control = ctrl_RIS)

aic_random <- AIC(fit_RI_fatigue, fit_RIS_fatigue)
print(aic_random)

best_random <- if (aic_random$AIC[1] <= aic_random$AIC[2]) "RI" else "RIS"
cat("\n>> Best random structure by AIC:", best_random, "\n")

## ---------- Step 2: Within chosen random structure, compare time effects ----------
if (best_random == "RI") {
  # Factor (response-profile)
  fit_factor <- fit_RI_fatigue

  # Linear time
  form_RI_linear <- as.formula(
    paste0("Fatigue ~ phase_num + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
           " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
  )
  fit_linear <- glmer(form_RI_linear, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

  # Quadratic time
  form_RI_quad <- as.formula(
    paste0("Fatigue ~ phase_num_c + I(phase_num_c^2) + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
           " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
  )
  fit_quadratic <- glmer(form_RI_quad, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

} else {  # RIS
  # Factor with random slope for phase (dummy slopes for T2, T3; ref T1)
  fit_factor <- fit_RIS_fatigue

  # Linear time with random slope on linear time
  form_RIS_linear <- as.formula(
    paste0("Fatigue ~ phase_num + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
           " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (phase_num | ID)")
  )
  fit_linear <- glmer(form_RIS_linear, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 1, control = ctrl_RIS)

  # Quadratic time: random slope for linear component only (more stable)
  form_RIS_quad <- as.formula(
    paste0("Fatigue ~ phase_num_c + I(phase_num_c^2) + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
           " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (phase_num_c | ID)")
  )
  fit_quadratic <- glmer(form_RIS_quad, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 1, control = ctrl_RIS)
}

# AIC table & choose best time specification
aic_time <- data.frame(
  TimeSpec = c("factor","linear","quadratic"),
  AIC      = c(AIC(fit_factor), AIC(fit_linear), AIC(fit_quadratic))
)
aic_time <- aic_time[order(aic_time$AIC), ]
cat("\n>> Time-effect AICs within chosen random structure:\n"); print(aic_time)

best_timespec <- aic_time$TimeSpec[1]
best_glmm <- switch(best_timespec,
  factor    = fit_factor,
  linear    = fit_linear,
  quadratic = fit_quadratic
)

cat("\n================ BEST GLMM (by AIC) ================\n")
cat("Random structure:", best_random, "\n")
cat("Time specification:", best_timespec, "\n")
cat("AIC:", AIC(best_glmm), "\n\n")
print(summary(best_glmm))

## ---------- Optional: pairwise phase contrasts if factor-time won ----------
if (best_timespec == "factor") {
  cat("\nPairwise phase contrasts (T2–T1, T3–T1, T3–T2) with Holm adjust:\n")
  emm <- emmeans(best_glmm, ~ phase)
  print(pairs(emm, adjust = "holm"))
}

## (Optional) restore previous contrast settings
options(op_old)



```

```{r,eval=FALSE}
library(performance)

# Assumptions for the selected Fatigue GLMM (RI or RIS, whichever won)
assess_fatigue <- check_glmm_assumptions(
  best_glmm,
  data = dat_long,
  do_plots = TRUE,        # show diagnostic plots
  do_sensitivity = TRUE   # optimizer/integration robustness
)

# Quick summary
assess_fatigue$overall_ok
assess_fatigue$sensitivity$agreement
```

2. Heatwave Fatigue LGME (RI Force)
```{r}
# ============================
# RI-only GLMM: select time effect (report FACTOR) → check assumptions → summarize
# Works for ANY binary outcome in dat_long (set `outcome_name`)
# ============================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(lme4)
  library(emmeans)      # for phase contrasts (factor time)
  library(performance)  # for check_glmm_assumptions()
})

## --- Choose outcome here ---
outcome_name <- "Fatigue"   # <-- change to "Dyspnea", "Affected", etc.

## --- Prep ---
stopifnot(all(c(outcome_name, "phase", "ID") %in% names(dat_long)))

# Robust 0/1 coercion for the binary outcome
to01 <- function(x){
  if (is.logical(x)) return(as.integer(x))
  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    ux <- sort(unique(x)); if (all(ux %in% c(0,1))) return(as.integer(x))
    stop("Numeric outcome must be coded 0/1; found: ", paste(ux, collapse=", "))
  }
  if (is.factor(x) || is.character(x)) {
    z <- tolower(trimws(as.character(x)))
    ones  <- c("1","yes","y","true","t","present","pos","positive")
    zeros <- c("0","no","n","false","f","absent","neg","negative")
    out <- ifelse(z %in% ones, 1L, ifelse(z %in% zeros, 0L, NA_integer_))
    if (any(is.na(out))) stop("Unrecognized outcome levels for 0/1 mapping: ",
                              paste(sort(unique(z[is.na(out)])), collapse=", "))
    return(out)
  }
  stop("Unsupported outcome type: ", class(x)[1])
}

d <- dat_long
d[[outcome_name]] <- to01(d[[outcome_name]])

# Phase as categorical T1/T2/T3 (no L/Q), T1 baseline
d$phase <- factor(d$phase, levels = c("T1","T2","T3"), ordered = FALSE)
contrasts(d$phase) <- contr.treatment(3, base = 1)

# Be tolerant to alternate column names
fam_sz <- if ("Family_Size_Group" %in% names(d)) "Family_Size_Group" else "Family_Size"
inc    <- if ("Monthly_Income_Group" %in% names(d)) "Monthly_Income_Group" else "Monthly_Income"

# Numeric encodings for time (for linear/quadratic candidates)
d$phase_num   <- as.numeric(d$phase) - 1         # 0,1,2
d$phase_num_c <- scale(d$phase_num, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)

ctrl_RI <- glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))

## --- RI models with different time effects ---

# 1) Factor (response profile: T2/T3 vs T1)
form_RI_factor <- as.formula(
  paste0(outcome_name, " ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
)
fit_RI_factor <- glmer(form_RI_factor, family = binomial, data = d,
                       nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

# 2) Linear time (0→1→2)
form_RI_linear <- as.formula(
  paste0(outcome_name, " ~ phase_num + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
)
fit_RI_linear <- glmer(form_RI_linear, family = binomial, data = d,
                       nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

# 3) Quadratic time (centered linear + square)
form_RI_quad <- as.formula(
  paste0(outcome_name, " ~ phase_num_c + I(phase_num_c^2) + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
)
fit_RI_quad <- glmer(form_RI_quad, family = binomial, data = d,
                     nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

## --- AIC table for time specs (RI only) ---
aic_tab <- data.frame(
  TimeSpec = c("factor","linear","quadratic"),
  AIC      = c(AIC(fit_RI_factor), AIC(fit_RI_linear), AIC(fit_RI_quad))
)
aic_tab <- aic_tab[order(aic_tab$AIC), ]
cat("\n=== AIC: RI time specifications (", outcome_name, ") ===\n", sep = ""); print(aic_tab)

## --- Forced selection: always use FACTOR time model ---
best_timespec <- "factor"
best_glmm_RI  <- fit_RI_factor
cat("\n>> Reporting RI + FACTOR time model (", outcome_name, ")\n", sep = "")

## --- Assumption checks on the selected model ---
assess_RI <- check_glmm_assumptions(
  best_glmm_RI,
  data = d,
  do_plots = TRUE,
  do_sensitivity = TRUE
)
cat("\nAssumption check — overall OK? ", assess_RI$overall_ok, "\n")
cat("Sensitivity agreement (GLM-CRSE, GEE):\n")
print(assess_RI$sensitivity$agreement)

## --- Final summary of the selected model ---
cat("\n=== Summary of the selected RI model (", outcome_name, ") ===\n", sep = "")
print(summary(best_glmm_RI))

## --- Pairwise phase contrasts (log-odds + OR with 95% CI) ---
cat("\nPairwise phase contrasts (log-odds), Holm-adjusted:\n")
emm_fac <- emmeans(best_glmm_RI, ~ phase)
print(pairs(emm_fac, adjust = "holm"))

cat("\nSame contrasts as odds ratios (95% CI):\n")
print(summary(pairs(emm_fac, adjust = "holm"), type = "response", infer = c(TRUE, TRUE)))


```













2. Heatwave Fatigue GEE
```{r}
# ===========================
# GEE selection + assumptions (Fatigue outcome) - T1/T2/T3 coding
# ===========================
suppressPackageStartupMessages({ library(geepack); library(emmeans) })

# ---- 0) Clean & sort --------------------------------------------
vars <- c("Fatigue","phase","Age_Group","Gender","Family_Size_Group",
          "Monthly_Income_Group","HH_Area","HTN_prior","Asthma_prior",
          "Thyroid_prior","ID")

d <- na.omit(dat_long[, vars])
d <- droplevels(d)
stopifnot(!anyNA(d$ID))

# Robust 0/1 coercion for the binary outcome
to01 <- function(x){
  if (is.logical(x)) return(as.integer(x))
  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    ux <- sort(unique(x))
    if (all(ux %in% c(0,1))) return(as.integer(x))
    stop("Numeric Fatigue must be coded 0/1; found: ", paste(ux, collapse=", "))
  }
  if (is.factor(x) || is.character(x)) {
    z <- tolower(trimws(as.character(x)))
    ones <- c("1","yes","y","true","t","present","pos","positive")
    zeros <- c("0","no","n","false","f","absent","neg","negative")
    out <- ifelse(z %in% ones, 1L, ifelse(z %in% zeros, 0L, NA_integer_))
    if (any(is.na(out))) {
      bad <- sort(unique(z[is.na(out)]))
      stop("Unrecognized Fatigue levels for 0/1 mapping: ", paste(bad, collapse=", "))
    }
    return(out)
  }
  stop("Unsupported Fatigue type: ", class(x)[1])
}

d$Fatigue <- to01(d$Fatigue)

# >>> IMPORTANT: use treatment contrasts (T1 reference), NOT polynomial L/Q
d$phase <- factor(d$phase, levels = c("T1","T2","T3"), ordered = FALSE)
# options(contrasts = c("contr.treatment","contr.poly"))  # optional

# Sort so AR(1) can infer spacing by row order (if chosen)
d <- d[order(d$ID, d$phase), ]

# Common formula (same covariates as your GLMM)
form <- Fatigue ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + Family_Size_Group + Monthly_Income_Group +
  HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior

# ---- 1) Fit grid: working correlation × link ---------------------
cor_opts <- c("independence","exchangeable","ar1","unstructured")
families <- list(
  logit   = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),
  probit  = stats::binomial(link = "probit"),
  cloglog = stats::binomial(link = "cloglog")
)

fits <- list(); rows <- list()
for (cc in cor_opts) {
  for (lname in names(families)) {
    fam <- families[[lname]]
    key <- paste(cc, lname, sep = "_")
    fit <- geeglm(form, id = ID, family = fam, corstr = cc, data = d)
    qic <- geepack::QIC(fit)
    fits[[key]] <- fit
    rows[[key]] <- data.frame(Correlation = cc, Link = lname,
                              QIC = unname(qic[["QIC"]]),
                              QICu = if (!is.null(qic[["QICu"]])) unname(qic[["QICu"]]) else NA_real_)
  }
}
sel_tab <- do.call(rbind, rows)
row.names(sel_tab) <- NULL
sel_tab <- sel_tab[order(sel_tab$QIC), ]
cat("\nModel selection grid (lower QIC is better):\n"); print(sel_tab)

best_idx  <- which.min(sel_tab$QIC)
best_cor  <- sel_tab$Correlation[best_idx]
best_link <- sel_tab$Link[best_idx]
fit_best  <- fits[[paste(best_cor, best_link, sep = "_")]]

cat("\nBest: correlation =", best_cor, ", link =", best_link,
    " (QIC =", round(sel_tab$QIC[best_idx], 3),
    if (!is.na(sel_tab$QICu[best_idx])) paste0(", QICu = ", round(sel_tab$QICu[best_idx], 3)) else "",
    ")\n")

# ---- 2) Slide-style table (logit only) ---------------------------
if (!is.null(fits[["unstructured_logit"]])) {
  qic_log <- c(
    Unstructured              = geepack::QIC(fits[["unstructured_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    `Constant (Exchangeable)` = geepack::QIC(fits[["exchangeable_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    `AR(1)`                   = geepack::QIC(fits[["ar1_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    Independence              = geepack::QIC(fits[["independence_logit"]])[["QIC"]]
  )
  pres_tab <- data.frame(Correlation = names(qic_log),
                         `QIC (logit)` = round(unname(qic_log), 1),
                         check.names = FALSE)
  cat("\nWorking correlation comparison (logit only):\n"); print(pres_tab)
}

# ---- 3) Diagnostics ----------------------------------------------
# 3a) Clusters & size
n_clusters <- length(unique(d$ID))
cat("\n# Clusters:", n_clusters, "\n")
cat("Cluster-size distribution:\n"); print(table(table(d$ID)))

# 3b) Within-ID variation for 'phase'
wcv <- tapply(d$phase, d$ID, function(x) length(unique(x)))
cat("Proportion of clusters with >1 phase level:", round(mean(wcv > 1), 3), "\n")

# 3c) Sparse cells / separation check for phase
tab_phase <- xtabs(~ Fatigue + phase, data = d)
cat("\nContingency of Fatigue by phase:\n"); print(tab_phase)

# 3d) Overdispersion (binomial quasi-likelihood)
phi <- sum(residuals(fit_best, type = "pearson")^2) / (nrow(d) - length(coef(fit_best)))
cat("\nPearson scale (phi):", round(phi, 3), " ( >1 suggests overdispersion )\n")

# 3e) Estimated working correlation
if (!is.null(fit_best$geese$alpha)) {
  cat("Estimated working correlation (alpha):", round(fit_best$geese$alpha, 3), "\n")
}

# 3f) Influence: leave-one-ID-out on first 'phase' term (T2 vs T1 coefficient)
phase_terms <- grep("^phase", names(coef(fit_best)), value = TRUE)
if (length(phase_terms) > 0) {
  target   <- phase_terms[1]   # typically "phaseT2" if T1 is reference
  base_est <- coef(fit_best)[target]
  ids <- unique(d$ID)
  set.seed(1)
  probe_ids <- if (length(ids) > 80) sample(ids, 80) else ids

  loo_delta <- sapply(probe_ids, function(i){
    dd <- d[d$ID != i, ]
    dd <- dd[order(dd$ID, dd$phase), ]
    fam_best <- families[[best_link]]
    fit_i <- geeglm(form, id = ID, family = fam_best, corstr = best_cor, data = dd)
    cf <- coef(fit_i); if (!target %in% names(cf)) return(NA_real_)
    cf[target] - base_est
  })
  infl_tab <- data.frame(ID = probe_ids, dBeta = loo_delta)
  infl_tab <- infl_tab[order(abs(infl_tab$dBeta), decreasing = TRUE), ]
  cat("\nTop 10 influential clusters for", target, "(|Δβ|):\n")
  print(head(infl_tab, 10), row.names = FALSE)
}

# ---- 4) Effects table with robust CIs ----------------------------
est <- coef(fit_best)
se  <- sqrt(diag(fit_best$geese$vbeta))     # robust (sandwich)
z   <- est / se
p   <- 2*pnorm(abs(z), lower.tail = FALSE)

if (best_link == "logit") {
  OUT <- data.frame(
    Variable = names(est),
    OR  = round(exp(est), 3),
    LCL = round(exp(est - 1.96*se), 3),
    UCL = round(exp(est + 1.96*se), 3),
    `p(Wald)` = signif(p, 3),
    check.names = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
} else {
  OUT <- data.frame(
    Variable = names(est),
    Estimate = round(est, 3),
    LCL = round(est - 1.96*se, 3),  # keep on link scale
    UCL = round(est + 1.96*se, 3),
    `p(Wald)` = signif(p, 3),
    Link = best_link,
    check.names = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
}
cat("\nPopulation-averaged effects for best GEE model (Fatigue):\n"); print(OUT)

# ---- 5) Pairwise phase contrasts (T2–T1, T3–T1, T3–T2) -----------
emm  <- emmeans(fit_best, ~ phase, vcov. = vcov(fit_best))   # robust vcov from GEE
cmp  <- pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")
cat("\nPairwise phase contrasts (", best_link, " link):\n", sep = ""); print(summary(cmp))

# Also show odds ratios if the chosen link is logit
if (best_link == "logit") {
  sc <- summary(cmp)
  OR_tab <- transform(sc,
    OR  = exp(estimate),
    LCL = exp(estimate - 1.96*SE),
    UCL = exp(estimate + 1.96*SE)
  )[ , c("contrast","OR","LCL","UCL","p.value")]
  cat("\nPairwise odds ratios (logit link):\n"); print(OR_tab, row.names = FALSE)
}

```


2. Heatwave Fatigue GEE
```{r}
# From your selection step:
best_link <- "cloglog"
best_cor  <- "unstructured"

family_map <- list(
  logit   = binomial("logit"),
  probit  = binomial("probit"),
  cloglog = binomial("cloglog")
)

# Assumes you already have `d` and `form` defined with Fatigue as the outcome:
# form <- Fatigue ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + Family_Size_Group + Monthly_Income_Group +
#   HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior

best_gee_model <- geeglm(
  form,
  id     = ID,
  family = family_map[[best_link]],   # cloglog
  corstr = best_cor,                  # unstructured
  data   = d
)

summary(best_gee_model)



library(emmeans)

# Pairwise comparisons among T1, T2, T3
emm  <- emmeans(best_gee_model, ~ phase, vcov. = vcov(best_gee_model))  # robust vcov from GEE
pairs_phase <- pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")  # multiple-testing correction
summary(pairs_phase)


```




3.  Heatwave Headache LGME (RIS )
```{r,eval=FALSE}
## ============================
## GLMM selector for Headache (T1/T2/T3 coding)
## ============================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(lme4)
  library(splines)
  library(emmeans)   # optional; for pairwise phase contrasts if factor-time wins
})

## ---------- Prep ----------
stopifnot(all(c("Headache","phase","ID") %in% names(dat_long)))

# Ensure binary 0/1 and drop unused levels
dat_long$Headache <- as.integer(dat_long$Headache)
dat_long <- droplevels(dat_long)

# Phase as UNordered factor (no polynomial coding), T1 is reference
dat_long$phase <- factor(dat_long$phase, levels = c("T1","T2","T3"), ordered = FALSE)

# Force treatment contrasts globally (fixed & random parts)
op_old <- options(contrasts = c("contr.treatment","contr.treatment"))

# Be tolerant to either grouped or raw names for these covariates
fam_sz <- if ("Family_Size_Group" %in% names(dat_long)) "Family_Size_Group" else "Family_Size"
inc    <- if ("Monthly_Income_Group" %in% names(dat_long)) "Monthly_Income_Group" else "Monthly_Income"

# Numeric time encodings (for linear/quadratic candidate models)
dat_long$phase_num   <- as.numeric(dat_long$phase) - 1            # 0,1,2 with T1=0
dat_long$phase_num_c <- scale(dat_long$phase_num, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)

# Controls
ctrl_RI  <- glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
ctrl_RIS <- glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 5e5))

## ---------- Step 1: RI vs RIS using factor time ----------
form_RI_factor <- as.formula(
  paste0("Headache ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
)
fit_RI_headache <- glmer(form_RI_factor, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

# With treatment contrasts, (phase | ID) uses dummy slopes for T2, T3 (ref = T1) — no L/Q
form_RIS_factor <- as.formula(
  paste0("Headache ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (phase | ID)")
)
fit_RIS_headache <- glmer(form_RIS_factor, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 1, control = ctrl_RIS)

aic_random <- AIC(fit_RI_headache, fit_RIS_headache)
print(aic_random)

best_random <- if (aic_random$AIC[1] <= aic_random$AIC[2]) "RI" else "RIS"
cat("\n>> Best random structure by AIC:", best_random, "\n")

## ---------- Step 2: Within chosen random structure, compare time effects (FACTOR, LINEAR, QUADRATIC) ----------
if (best_random == "RI") {
  # (A) Factor (response-profile) under RI
  fit_factor <- fit_RI_headache

  # (B) Linear time under RI
  form_RI_linear <- as.formula(
    paste0("Headache ~ phase_num + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
           " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
  )
  fit_linear <- glmer(form_RI_linear, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

  # (C) Quadratic time under RI
  form_RI_quad <- as.formula(
    paste0("Headache ~ phase_num_c + I(phase_num_c^2) + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
           " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
  )
  fit_quadratic <- glmer(form_RI_quad, family = binomial, data = dat_long, nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

} else {  # ------ RIS branch: TEST ALL THREE (factor, linear, quadratic) ------

  # (A) Factor time with random slopes for phase dummies (ref = T1)
  form_RIS_factor <- as.formula(
    paste0("Headache ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
           " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (phase | ID)")
  )
  fit_RIS_factor <- glmer(form_RIS_factor, family = binomial, data = dat_long,
                          nAGQ = 1, control = ctrl_RIS)

  # (B) Linear time with random slope on linear time
  form_RIS_linear <- as.formula(
    paste0("Headache ~ phase_num + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
           " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (phase_num | ID)")
  )
  fit_RIS_linear <- glmer(form_RIS_linear, family = binomial, data = dat_long,
                          nAGQ = 1, control = ctrl_RIS)

  # (C) Quadratic time: random slope for the linear component only (more stable)
  form_RIS_quad <- as.formula(
    paste0("Headache ~ phase_num_c + I(phase_num_c^2) + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
           " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (phase_num_c | ID)")
  )
  fit_RIS_quadratic <- glmer(form_RIS_quad, family = binomial, data = dat_long,
                             nAGQ = 1, control = ctrl_RIS)

  # Align names to selector variables
  fit_factor    <- fit_RIS_factor
  fit_linear    <- fit_RIS_linear
  fit_quadratic <- fit_RIS_quadratic
}

# AIC table & choose best time specification (three models compared)
aic_time <- data.frame(
  TimeSpec = c("factor","linear","quadratic"),
  AIC      = c(AIC(fit_factor), AIC(fit_linear), AIC(fit_quadratic))
)
aic_time <- aic_time[order(aic_time$AIC), ]
cat("\n>> Time-effect AICs within chosen random structure:\n"); print(aic_time)

best_timespec <- aic_time$TimeSpec[1]
best_glmm <- switch(best_timespec,
  factor    = fit_factor,
  linear    = fit_linear,
  quadratic = fit_quadratic
)

cat("\n================ BEST GLMM (by AIC) ================\n")
cat("Random structure:", best_random, "\n")
cat("Time specification:", best_timespec, "\n")
cat("AIC:", AIC(best_glmm), "\n\n")
print(summary(best_glmm))

## ---------- Optional: pairwise phase contrasts if factor-time won ----------
if (best_timespec == "factor") {
  cat("\nPairwise phase contrasts (T2–T1, T3–T1, T3–T2) with Holm adjust:\n")
  emm <- emmeans(best_glmm, ~ phase)
  print(pairs(emm, adjust = "holm"))
}

## (Optional) restore previous contrast settings
options(op_old)



```



3.  Heatwave Headache LGME (RIS )
```{r,eval=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(performance))

# Assumptions for the selected Headache GLMM (RI or RIS, whichever your selector chose)
assess_headache <- check_glmm_assumptions(
  best_glmm,
  data = dat_long,
  do_plots = TRUE,        # show diagnostic plots
  do_sensitivity = TRUE   # optimizer/integration robustness
)

# Quick summary
assess_headache$overall_ok
assess_headache$sensitivity$agreement

```



3.  Heatwave Headache LGME (RI Force)
```{r}
# ============================
# RI-only GLMM (Headache outcome, heatwave data)
# - Fit RI models only
# - Compute AIC for factor/linear/quadratic
# - Always select FACTOR model
# ============================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(lme4)
  library(emmeans)      # for phase contrasts
  library(performance)  # for check_glmm_assumptions()
})

## --- Prep ---
stopifnot(all(c("Headache","phase","ID") %in% names(dat_long)))

# Robust 0/1 coercion
to01 <- function(x){
  if (is.logical(x)) return(as.integer(x))
  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    if (all(sort(unique(x)) %in% c(0,1))) return(as.integer(x))
    stop("Numeric Headache must be coded 0/1.")
  }
  if (is.factor(x) || is.character(x)) {
    z <- tolower(trimws(as.character(x)))
    ones  <- c("1","yes","y","true","t","present","pos","positive")
    zeros <- c("0","no","n","false","f","absent","neg","negative")
    out <- ifelse(z %in% ones, 1L, ifelse(z %in% zeros, 0L, NA_integer_))
    if (any(is.na(out))) stop("Unrecognized Headache values.")
    return(out)
  }
  stop("Unsupported Headache type: ", class(x)[1])
}
d <- dat_long
d$Headache <- to01(d$Headache)

# Phase as categorical T1/T2/T3 (factor baseline = T1)
d$phase <- factor(d$phase, levels = c("T1","T2","T3"), ordered = FALSE)
contrasts(d$phase) <- contr.treatment(3, base = 1)

# Allow grouped/raw family size & income
fam_sz <- if ("Family_Size_Group" %in% names(d)) "Family_Size_Group" else "Family_Size"
inc    <- if ("Monthly_Income_Group" %in% names(d)) "Monthly_Income_Group" else "Monthly_Income"

# Numeric encodings for time
d$phase_num   <- as.numeric(d$phase) - 1
d$phase_num_c <- scale(d$phase_num, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)

ctrl_RI <- glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))

## --- RI models with different time effects ---

# 1) Factor time
form_factor <- as.formula(
  paste0("Headache ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
)
fit_factor <- glmer(form_factor, family = binomial, data = d,
                    nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

# 2) Linear time
form_linear <- as.formula(
  paste0("Headache ~ phase_num + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
)
fit_linear <- glmer(form_linear, family = binomial, data = d,
                    nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

# 3) Quadratic time
form_quad <- as.formula(
  paste0("Headache ~ phase_num_c + I(phase_num_c^2) + Age_Group + Gender + ", fam_sz, " + ", inc,
         " + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior + (1 | ID)")
)
fit_quad <- glmer(form_quad, family = binomial, data = d,
                  nAGQ = 20, control = ctrl_RI)

## --- Compare AIC (for info only) ---
cat("\n=== AIC: RI time specifications (Headache) ===\n")
aic_tab <- data.frame(
  TimeSpec = c("factor","linear","quadratic"),
  AIC      = c(AIC(fit_factor), AIC(fit_linear), AIC(fit_quad))
)
print(aic_tab[order(aic_tab$AIC), ])

## --- Forced selection: always use FACTOR ---
best_glmm <- fit_factor
cat("\n>> Reporting RI + FACTOR time model (Headache)\n")

## --- Assumption checks ---
assess <- check_glmm_assumptions(
  best_glmm,
  data = d,
  do_plots = TRUE,
  do_sensitivity = TRUE
)
cat("\nAssumption check — overall OK? ", assess$overall_ok, "\n")
cat("Sensitivity agreement (GLM-CRSE, GEE):\n")
print(assess$sensitivity$agreement)

## --- Final summary ---
cat("\n=== Summary of the selected RI model (Headache) ===\n")
print(summary(best_glmm))

## --- Pairwise contrasts (always run, since factor forced) ---
cat("\nPairwise phase contrasts (log-odds), Holm-adjusted:\n")
emm_fac <- emmeans(best_glmm, ~ phase)
print(pairs(emm_fac, adjust = "holm"))

cat("\nSame contrasts as odds ratios (95% CI):\n")
print(summary(pairs(emm_fac, adjust = "holm"), type = "response", infer = c(TRUE, TRUE)))

```



















3.  Heatwave Headache GEE
```{r}
# ===========================
# GEE selection + assumptions (Headache outcome) - T1/T2/T3 coding
# ===========================
suppressPackageStartupMessages({ library(geepack); library(emmeans) })

# ---- 0) Clean & sort --------------------------------------------
# use HEATWAVE long data
base_df <- dat_long

# allow for grouped or raw versions of family size & income
fam_sz <- if ("Family_Size_Group" %in% names(base_df)) "Family_Size_Group" else "Family_Size"
inc    <- if ("Monthly_Income_Group" %in% names(base_df)) "Monthly_Income_Group" else "Monthly_Income"

vars <- c("Headache","phase","Age_Group","Gender", fam_sz, inc,
          "HH_Area","HTN_prior","Asthma_prior","Thyroid_prior","CVDs_prior","ID")

d <- na.omit(base_df[, vars])
d <- droplevels(d)
stopifnot(!anyNA(d$ID))

# Robust 0/1 coercion for the binary outcome
to01 <- function(x){
  if (is.logical(x)) return(as.integer(x))
  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    ux <- sort(unique(x))
    if (all(ux %in% c(0,1))) return(as.integer(x))
    stop("Numeric Headache must be coded 0/1; found: ", paste(ux, collapse=", "))
  }
  if (is.factor(x) || is.character(x)) {
    z <- tolower(trimws(as.character(x)))
    ones  <- c("1","yes","y","true","t","present","pos","positive")
    zeros <- c("0","no","n","false","f","absent","neg","negative")
    out <- ifelse(z %in% ones, 1L, ifelse(z %in% zeros, 0L, NA_integer_))
    if (any(is.na(out))) {
      bad <- sort(unique(z[is.na(out)]))
      stop("Unrecognized Headache levels for 0/1 mapping: ", paste(bad, collapse=", "))
    }
    return(out)
  }
  stop("Unsupported Headache type: ", class(x)[1])
}
d$Headache <- to01(d$Headache)

# Use treatment contrasts with T1 as reference (UNORDERED)
want_lvls <- c("T1","T2","T3")
if (!all(want_lvls %in% levels(d$phase))) {
  stop("phase must have levels T1, T2, T3; found: ", paste(levels(d$phase), collapse=", "))
}
d$phase <- factor(d$phase, levels = want_lvls, ordered = FALSE)
contrasts(d$phase) <- stats::contr.treatment(n = length(levels(d$phase)), base = 1)

# AR(1) wave index + sort
d$wave <- as.integer(d$phase)
d <- d[order(d$ID, d$phase), ]

# Common formula
form <- as.formula(sprintf(
  "Headache ~ phase + Age_Group + Gender + %s + %s + HH_Area + HTN_prior + Asthma_prior + Thyroid_prior + CVDs_prior",
  fam_sz, inc
))

# ---- 1) Fit grid: working correlation × link ---------------------
cor_opts <- c("independence","exchangeable","ar1","unstructured")
families <- list(
  logit   = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),
  probit  = stats::binomial(link = "probit"),
  cloglog = stats::binomial(link = "cloglog")
)

fits <- list(); rows <- list()
for (cc in cor_opts) {
  for (lname in names(families)) {
    fam <- families[[lname]]
    key <- paste(cc, lname, sep = "_")
    fit <- geeglm(form, id = ID, waves = wave, family = fam, corstr = cc, data = d)
    qic <- geepack::QIC(fit)
    fits[[key]] <- fit
    rows[[key]] <- data.frame(Correlation = cc, Link = lname,
                              QIC = unname(qic[["QIC"]]),
                              QICu = if (!is.null(qic[["QICu"]])) unname(qic[["QICu"]]) else NA_real_)
  }
}
sel_tab <- do.call(rbind, rows); row.names(sel_tab) <- NULL
sel_tab <- sel_tab[order(sel_tab$QIC), ]
cat("\nModel selection grid (lower QIC is better):\n"); print(sel_tab)

best_idx  <- which.min(sel_tab$QIC)
best_cor  <- sel_tab$Correlation[best_idx]
best_link <- sel_tab$Link[best_idx]
best_gee_model <- fits[[paste(best_cor, best_link, sep = "_")]]
cat("\nBest: correlation =", best_cor, ", link =", best_link,
    " (QIC =", round(sel_tab$QIC[best_idx], 3),
    if (!is.na(sel_tab$QICu[best_idx])) paste0(", QICu = ", round(sel_tab$QICu[best_idx], 3)) else "",
    ")\n")

# Show full model summary
cat("\n=== Summary of the best GEE model (Headache) ===\n")
print(summary(best_gee_model))

# ---- 2) Slide-style table (logit only) ---------------------------
if (!is.null(fits[["unstructured_logit"]])) {
  qic_log <- c(
    Unstructured              = geepack::QIC(fits[["unstructured_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    `Constant (Exchangeable)` = geepack::QIC(fits[["exchangeable_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    `AR(1)`                   = geepack::QIC(fits[["ar1_logit"]])[["QIC"]],
    Independence              = geepack::QIC(fits[["independence_logit"]])[["QIC"]]
  )
  pres_tab <- data.frame(Correlation = names(qic_log),
                         `QIC (logit)` = round(unname(qic_log), 1),
                         check.names = FALSE)
  cat("\nWorking correlation comparison (logit only):\n"); print(pres_tab)
}

# ---- 3) Diagnostics ----------------------------------------------
n_clusters <- length(unique(d$ID))
cat("\n# Clusters:", n_clusters, "\n")
cat("Cluster-size distribution:\n"); print(table(table(d$ID)))

wcv <- tapply(d$phase, d$ID, function(x) length(unique(x)))
cat("Proportion of clusters with >1 phase level:", round(mean(wcv > 1), 3), "\n")

tab_phase <- xtabs(~ Headache + phase, data = d)
cat("\nContingency of Headache by phase:\n"); print(tab_phase)
if (any(apply(tab_phase, 2, function(col) any(col == 0)))) {
  warning("Zero cell(s) detected for some phase levels (check separation).")
}

phi <- sum(residuals(best_gee_model, type = "pearson")^2) / (nrow(d) - length(coef(best_gee_model)))
cat("\nPearson scale (phi):", round(phi, 3), " ( >1 suggests overdispersion )\n")

if (!is.null(best_gee_model$geese$alpha)) {
  cat("Estimated working correlation (alpha):", round(best_gee_model$geese$alpha, 3), "\n")
}

phase_terms <- grep("^phase", names(coef(best_gee_model)), value = TRUE)
if (length(phase_terms) > 0) {
  target   <- phase_terms[1]
  base_est <- coef(best_gee_model)[target]
  ids <- unique(d$ID)
  set.seed(1)
  probe_ids <- if (length(ids) > 80) sample(ids, 80) else ids

  loo_delta <- sapply(probe_ids, function(i){
    dd <- d[d$ID != i, ]
    dd <- dd[order(dd$ID, dd$phase), ]
    fam_best <- families[[best_link]]
    fit_i <- geeglm(form, id = ID, waves = wave, family = fam_best, corstr = best_cor, data = dd)
    cf <- coef(fit_i); if (!target %in% names(cf)) return(NA_integer_)
    cf[target] - base_est
  })
  infl_tab <- data.frame(ID = probe_ids, dBeta = loo_delta)
  infl_tab <- infl_tab[order(abs(infl_tab$dBeta), decreasing = TRUE), ]
  cat("\nTop 10 influential clusters for", target, "(|Δβ|):\n")
  print(head(infl_tab, 10), row.names = FALSE)
}

# ---- 4) Effects table with robust CIs ----------------------------
est <- coef(best_gee_model)
se  <- sqrt(diag(best_gee_model$geese$vbeta))
z   <- est / se
p   <- 2*pnorm(abs(z), lower.tail = FALSE)

if (best_link == "logit") {
  OUT <- data.frame(
    Variable = names(est),
    OR  = round(exp(est), 3),
    LCL = round(exp(est - 1.96*se), 3),
    UCL = round(exp(est + 1.96*se), 3),
    `p(Wald)` = signif(p, 3),
    check.names = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
} else {
  OUT <- data.frame(
    Variable = names(est),
    Estimate = round(est, 3),
    LCL = round(est - 1.96*se, 3),
    UCL = round(est + 1.96*se, 3),
    `p(Wald)` = signif(p, 3),
    Link = best_link,
    check.names = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
}
cat("\nPopulation-averaged effects for best GEE model (Headache):\n"); print(OUT)

# ---- 5) Pairwise phase contrasts (T2–T1, T3–T1, T3–T2) -----------
emm  <- emmeans(best_gee_model, ~ phase, vcov. = vcov(best_gee_model))
cmp  <- pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")
cat("\nPairwise phase contrasts (", best_link, " link):\n", sep = ""); print(summary(cmp))

if (best_link == "logit") {
  sc <- summary(cmp)
  OR_tab <- transform(sc,
    OR  = exp(estimate),
    LCL = exp(estimate - 1.96*SE),
    UCL = exp(estimate + 1.96*SE)
```
